title: Thread Suspension Mechanisms in Java
description: A flowchart illustrating the deprecated suspend() method versus the recommended wait/notify pattern for controlling thread execution.
relationships: Caller invokes suspend() on Target Thread -> Target Thread is paused unsafely (holding locks)
Caller invokes resume() -> Target Thread continues execution
Target Thread acquires lock and calls wait() -> releases lock and suspends itself safely
Another thread calls notify()/notifyAll() on the same lock -> awakens the waiting thread
